metadata.description: One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) tax provisions relevant to retirement and financial planning. P.L. 119-21, signed July 4, 2025.
metadata.law_reference: One Big Beautiful Bill Act, P.L. 119-21, 119th Congress
irs_guidance: Revenue Procedure 2025-32 (2026 inflation adjustments incorporating OBBBA)
FS-2025-03 (Tax deductions for workers and seniors)

tcja_permanence.description: OBBBA permanently extends individual tax provisions from the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017 that were set to expire December 31, 2025.

provisions_made_permanent.description: Seven-bracket structure with rates 10%, 12%, 22%, 24%, 32%, 35%, 37% made permanent
Enlarged standard deduction made permanent with additional OBBBA inflation adjustment for 2026 bottom two brackets
provisions_made_permanent.note: OBBBA provides 4% inflation adjustment for 10% and 12% brackets, 2.3% for higher brackets in 2026

provisions_made_permanent.description: Doubled estate tax exemption made permanent and indexed for inflation
CTC increased to $2,200 per child and made permanent, refundable portion $1,400 indexed for inflation
20% qualified business income deduction for pass-through businesses made permanent
Higher AMT exemption amounts made permanent and indexed for inflation

senior_deduction.description: Additional $6,000 deduction for taxpayers age 65 and older, available to both itemizers and non-itemizers, in addition to existing age 65+ additional standard deduction
senior_deduction.age_requirement: Must attain age 65 on or before last day of taxable year
senior_deduction.retirement_planning_note: Stacks with existing additional standard deduction for age 65+ ($2,000 single / $1,600 MFJ in 2026). Combined potential deduction reduction for qualifying single senior: up to $8,000 beyond base standard deduction.

tip_income_deduction.description: Deduction for qualified tips in customarily-tipped occupations

overtime_deduction.description: Deduction for qualified overtime compensation exceeding regular rate of pay

auto_loan_interest_deduction.description: Deduction for interest paid on loan for new personal-use vehicle
restrictions: New vehicle only (original use must begin with taxpayer)
Personal use only (not business or commercial)

salt_deduction.description: State and local tax deduction cap increased from $10,000 to $40,000
salt_deduction.note: Cap and income threshold increase 1% annually through 2029. After 2029, reverts to $10,000 unless extended.

trump_accounts.description: New tax-advantaged savings accounts for children under IRC Section 530A. Functions as traditional IRA during growth period (before age 18), then converts to standard IRA rules.

beneficiary_requirements: Under age 18 by end of calendar year

investment_restrictions: Mutual funds or ETFs tracking qualified U.S. equity index
Annual fees and expenses must not exceed 0.1% of balance

distribution_rules.growth_period_withdrawals: Generally prohibited until January 1 of year beneficiary turns 18
distribution_rules.post_18_rules: Converts to traditional IRA rules, distributions taxed as ordinary income

tax_treatment.distributions: Taxed as ordinary income under traditional IRA rules

energy_credit_changes.description: OBBBA phases out or eliminates several clean energy tax credits from the Inflation Reduction Act

provisions.description: 30% credit for rooftop solar and clean energy systems terminated
Credits up to $3,200 for efficiency upgrades terminated

energy_credit_changes.retirement_planning_note: Homeowners planning energy upgrades as part of retirement preparation should note these credits are no longer available for projects after the listed dates.

research_expensing.description: Domestic research and experimentation costs can be expensed in year incurred (retroactive to 2022 for small businesses, 2025 for others)

aca_premium_tax_credits.description: Enhanced ACA marketplace premium tax credits not extended; revert to pre-enhancement levels
aca_premium_tax_credits.note: May affect early retirees relying on ACA marketplace coverage before Medicare eligibility

medicare_physician_fee.description: Physician fee schedule increased 2.5% for services January 1, 2026 through December 31, 2026

hsa_eligibility_expansion.description: Individual market bronze and catastrophic health plans treated as high-deductible health plans eligible for HSA contributions
hsa_eligibility_expansion.retirement_planning_note: Expands HSA eligibility for early retirees on ACA marketplace bronze plans

cross_references.note: The following repo files contain 2026 values that already incorporate OBBBA changes via IRS Revenue Procedure 2025-32

files_incorporating_obbba.status: Current — reflects OBBBA-permanent TCJA rates with enhanced inflation adjustment
Current — $16,100 single / $32,200 MFJ reflects OBBBA adjustment
Current — OBBBA makes TCJA doubled exemption permanent
Current — OBBBA preserves TCJA capital gains rate structure
Current — note new OBBBA bronze plan HSA eligibility expansion
